alerts.title: Qatar Airways Passenger Support and Operational Updates
alerts.raw_body:
Qatar Airways continues to support passengers affected by recent operational disruptions beyond our control. Customers stranded in Doha have been provided with accommodation and on-the-ground assistance, while our teams in Doha and across our network continue to assist customers with rebooking requests.

Limited Passenger Flight Schedule

A limited schedule of passenger flights to/from Doha (DOH) are available to book via the Qatar Airways website, and mobile app. You can check our latest flight schedule at http://qatarairways.com/schedule

Qatar Airways is also operating selected non-stop point-to-point services, which have been arranged to help passengers complete their journeys. Customers eligible for these services will be contacted directly by Qatar Airways.

Operational Conditions

Flights are currently operating within a limited safe corridor defined by the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority. Therefore, the number of flights that can operate each day are extremely limited under the current operational conditions. Each flight requires careful planning and remains subject to regulatory approvals and airspace conditions.

Travel and Booking Information

The quickest way to manage your booking is online or on our app. If you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 28 March 2026, you're eligible for a refund or date change.

*   Two complimentary date changes to a new travel date up to 30 April 2026 when rebooking on flights operated by Qatar Airways, or

*   A refundof the unused ticket value of your ticket

*   Ensure your contact details are up to date to receive important notifications.

To modify your booking, visit http://qatarairways.com/manage

Important: Please travel to the airport only if you hold a valid confirmed ticket.

Customer Call Centre Update

Due to the ongoing disruption, we are currently experiencing exceptionally high call volumes. Customers are encouraged to check the latest flight status and booking related information (rebooking, refunds etc.) on the Qatar Airways website or mobile app.

For passenger FAQs and support guidance, visit:qatarairways.com/LatestFAQs

For operational updates, visit our travel alert page:qatarairways.com/travelalerts
alerts.summary: Flights to and from Doha are currently operating on a very limited schedule due to regulatory restrictions that are in place until 28 March 2026, with only a few secure corridors available. Passengers with confirmed bookings between 28 February and 28 March 2026 can either change their flight to a new date before 30 April 2026 or receive a refund of the unused ticket value, and they should check their booking status or initiate changes online via the Qatar Airways website or app. If you have been stranded in Doha, you have been provided with accommodation and assistance, and you should remain in contact with Qatar Airways for further support.

alerts.title: Qatar Airways Operates Limited Flight Schedule to and from Doha
alerts.raw_body:
With Qatar Airways scheduled flight operations still temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace, we want you to know that we are doing our utmost to support you with your journey, and to reunite you with your family and loved ones.

We recognise this situation may be unsettling and are working to keep you moving while we prepare for the safe return of our usual operations. Your patience and understanding mean a great deal to us, and we are truly grateful for your continued support at this challenging time. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe full reopening of Qatari airspace.

Qatar Airways will operate a revised limited number of flights from 18 March to 28 March 2026. These schedules have been enhanced to give more flexibility to passengers wishing to travel.

To view this schedule click here.

To book these flights and for the latest flight updates, please visit the Qatar Airways website or App, or contact a travel agent.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to one of the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated.

Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel.

The safety and wellbeing of our passengers and crew remain our highest priority during this period of disruption. We apologise for the inconvenience caused by the current situation, which is beyond our control, and thank our passengers for their patience and understanding.

As a continuing measure, please be reminded that if you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 28 March 2026, you are eligible for:

*   Two complimentary date changes to a new travel date up to 30 April 2026 when rebooking on flights operated by Qatar Airways, or
*   Refundof the unused ticket value

For booking related assistance, find out more by reading our FAQs.
alerts.summary: Qatar Airways flight operations to and from Doha are temporarily suspended while Qatari airspace remains closed, but the airline will run a limited revised schedule from 18 March to 28 March 2026. Passengers with confirmed bookings for those dates will receive new flight information; they should check the Qatar Airways website or app for updates, confirm their contact details, and either rebook using the available limited flights or request a refund or two complimentary date changes up to 30 April 2026.

alerts.summary: Qatar Airways is operating a limited flight schedule to and from Doha until 28 March 2026 because Qatari airspace remains closed, so only the routes listed in the revised timetable will run. Passengers with bookings for these destinations should check the Qatar Airways website or app for new flight details, update their contact information, and either rebook or request a refund before the September 30, 2026 deadline. If you have a confirmed ticket between 28 Feb and 30 Apr 2026, you may change your flight twice for a new date up to 31 May 2026 or obtain a refund of the unused ticket value.

alerts.title: 26 March 2026: Qatar Airways Continues to Restore Flight Network
alerts.raw_body:
Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to and from Doha. The latest update, valid until 15 April 2026, provides additional frequencies to more than 90 destinations across Qatar Airways’ global network.

All flights to and from Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors. These routes are established in close coordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

For more details, clickhere.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to one of the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated.

Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel.

Please note:Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

Our teams are doing everything within the current constraints to ensure our passengers reach their destinations. We apologise for any inconvenience caused during this period of disruption and thank passengers for their patience and understanding.

The safety and wellbeing of our passengers and crew remain our highest priority.

As a continuing measure, please be reminded that if you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 15 June 2026, you are eligible for:

· Complimentary date changes to a new travel date up to 31 October 2026 when rebooking on flights operated by Qatar Airways, subject to availability and fare seasonality, or

·Refundof the unused ticket value. Please note that refunds may take up to 28 days to be processed.

For booking related assistance, find out more by reading ourFAQ.
alerts.summary: Qatar Airways is restoring its flight network, but flights to and from Doha are still operating only through dedicated corridors coordinated with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority. All passengers with confirmed bookings for any of the newly scheduled destinations between 28 February and 15 June 2026 will receive updated travel details; they should check the Qatar Airways website or app, verify their contact information, and, if needed, rebook or request a refund (refunds may take up to 28 days). Passengers are advised not to arrive at the departure airport unless they have a valid, confirmed ticket for the updated flight.

alerts.title: Qatar Airways Further Rebuilds Flight Schedules
alerts.raw_body:
Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to, and from, Doha to more than 120 destinations by mid-May 2026 across the airline’s global network.

All flights to, and from, Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors established in close co-ordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

To view the schedule, click here.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information.Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated.

Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel.

Our teams are doing everything within the current constraints to ensure our passengers reach their destinations. We deeply apologise for any inconvenience caused during this period of disruption and thank our passengers for their patience and understanding.

The safety and wellbeing of our passengers and crew remain our highest priority.

As a continuing measure, please be reminded that if you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 15 June 2026,you are eligible for:

*   Complimentary date changes to a new travel date up to 31 October 2026 when rebooking on flights operated by Qatar Airways, subject to availability and fare seasonality, or

*   Refundof the unused ticket value. Please note that refunds may take up to 28 working days to be processed.

For booking related assistance, find out more by reading our FAQs.
alerts.summary: Qatar Airways is updating its flight schedules, gradually adding more flights to and from Doha to over 120 destinations, and all current flights to and from Doha will still depart via the established corridors with the Civil Aviation Authority. Passengers with confirmed bookings for travel between 28 February and 15 June 2026 should check their current flight status and update their contact details on the Qatar Airways website or app, as they may receive new flight information, can request a complimentary date change up to 31 October 2026, or receive a refund of the unused ticket value, which may take up to 28 working days. If you do not hold a valid, confirmed ticket, do not arrive at the departure airport.

alerts.title: Effective 12 April 2026: Changes to Travelling with Power Banks, E Cigarettes, and Vapes
alerts.raw_body:
Your safety is our priority. When travelling with Qatar Airways, passengers are permitted to carry power banks, e‑cigarettes, and vapes on board. However, as these electronic devices contain lithium batteries, the following important safety instructions must be observed at all times:

*   A maximum of two (2) power banks per passenger is permitted.
*   Each power bank must not exceed 100Wh. Further details can be found via IATA – Changes Regarding Power Banks.
*   Do not travel with any lithium battery‑powered equipment or power banksthat are, or have been, damaged orrecalled by authorities or manufacturersfor safety reasons. This also includes the use of damaged or faulty charging cables on board.
*   Where available, use in‑seat USB ports or power sockets to charge portable electronic devices. However, power banks, e‑cigarettes or vapes must not be charged on board.
*   Always apply safety caps or cover air vents on e‑cigarettes or vapes to prevent accidental activation.
*   When stored in your carry‑on baggage, devices containinglithium batteries and loose batteries must be kept away from flammable items, such as perfumes.
*   Do not move your seat, if a device is dropped or becomes lodged in your seat.
*   Immediately inform a member of the cabin crew, who will be happy to assist you in retrieving it safely.
*   Immediately notify cabin crew if your device overheats, emits smoke or fumes, or catches fire.

When using a power bank to charge other allowed electronic devices, it must remain in view. Do not place them inside your carry-on bag or in the overhead bins while in use.

Thank you for helping us keep everyone, including yourself, safe on every journey.
alerts.summary: Passengers on Qatar Airways flights may now bring up to two power banks, e-cigarettes, and vapes that are under 100 Wh; the items must not be charged on the aircraft and all lithium-battery devices must be kept in view while in use. This rule applies to all routes and dates from 12 April 2026 onward, so travelers should review their carry-on policy before departure, ensure they are not carrying damaged or recalled devices, and keep the batteries separated from flammable items in their luggage.

alerts.title: Frankfurt Airport Terminal Change
alerts.raw_body: From 23 April 2026, Qatar Airways operations at Frankfurt Airport will be handled exclusively from the new Terminal 3. Check‑in and boarding gates will no longer operate from Terminal 2, and all passengers should proceed directly to Terminal 3 for Qatar Airways flights.
alerts.summary: From April 23 2026 onwards, all Qatar Airways flights operating out of Frankfurt Airport will use Terminal 3 only; terminals 2 check-in and boarding gates will be closed. Passengers flying with Qatar Airways on or after that date should go directly to Terminal 3 for check-in and boarding. If you need to confirm your flight details or make changes, please check the Qatar Airways website or your booking confirmation for updated gate information.

alerts.title: Qatar Airways Expands Global Network to over 150 Destinations
alerts.raw_body:
Qatar Airways is expanding its international flight network, with services to over 150 destinations from 16 June 2026, connecting more passengers to more of the world this summer.

The updated schedule, valid until 15 September 2026, introduces new routes and increased frequencies to and from Doha, offering passengers greater flexibility as they plan for the summer season.

To view the schedule, clickhere.

Passengers with confirmed bookings on a flight in the new schedule will be notified directly with updated flight information. Qatar Airways recommends checking its website or app regularly and ensuring that contact details are kept up to date.

As a reminder, if you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 15 September 2026,you are eligible for:

· Complimentary date changes to a new travel date up to 31 October 2026 when rebooking on flights operated by Qatar Airways, subject to availability and fare seasonality.

· If your flight is impacted, you remain eligible for further fee-free changes until 31 October 2026, or

·Refundof the unused ticket value (Please note that refunds may take up to 28 working days to be processed).

Passengers are kindly reminded not to proceed to their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel.

Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation owing to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

For further assistance, please visit our FAQs.
alerts.summary: Qatar Airways is adding over 150 destinations starting 16 June 2026, with a revised schedule that remains in effect until 15 September 2026, and will issue personalized updates to passengers holding bookings under the new timetable. If you have a confirmed booking between 28 February and 15 September 2026, you can change your travel date for free to any flight operated by Qatar Airways until 31 October 2026 and you may also request a refund of the unused ticket value, which could take up to 28 working days. Check the official timetable, keep your contact details current, and verify your ticket status on the Qatar Airways website or app to avoid travelling without a valid confirmed ticket.
